title: Pilates with Cathy Raymond
description:
Pilates is a progressive system of exercises designed to stretch, strengthen, and balance both mind and body. Benefits include increased core strength, better posture and balance skills, more physical and mental energy, and improved sports performance.

A recording of each class is available on YouTube.

title: Japanese Language Table
description: Please join us for the Japanese Language Table.

title: Arabic Language Table
description: The Arabic Language Table provides a relaxed, supportive space for students to practice conversational Arabic, build confidence, and engage with the language beyond the classroom. It fosters spontaneous speech, reinforces vocabulary in context, and encourages cultural exploration through discussions on music, films, and current events. By creating a sense of community, it connects students across proficiency levels, motivating them to participate and apply their skills in real-world interactions. This informal setting helps bridge classroom learning with practical use, preparing students for study abroad, internships, and conversations with native speakers. Ultimately, it enhances fluency, cultural awareness, and a deeper appreciation for the Arabic language.
location: Dining Center 208 - Martin Low '40 Dining Room

title: GEO Post Application Meeting for Fall 2026
description: All Study Abroad applicants and admitted students must attend a mandatory “Post-Application Meeting.”  These sessions will cover what comes next - including the credit approval process, booking flights, insurance, visas, and other important topics.

title: Aydelotte Foundation Open Office Hours
description:
Aydelotte Foundation Director Patricia White and Senior Associate Director Andy Hines welcome you to drop in, say hello, and learn more about what we’re up to at our weekly open office hours on Wednesdays from 1-2pm in Parrish W201. Tea, coffee, and snacks will be available too!

Check out the Get Involved page, Publications, and the rest of the Aydelotte Foundation website to learn more about some of the things we've been working on.

If you can’t make it to office hours and would like to meet, feel free to reach out to us for an appointment at another time.

title: Lenape: Resilience, Return, and Renewal
description:
Jeremy Johnson, Cultural Education Director, Delaware Tribe of Indians (Lenape)


Scheuer Room, Kohlberg Hall

April 29, 4:30-6:00 p.m.

Description:

This is the fourth and final event in a series on the renaming of Old Science Hall.

In this talk, Johnson examines the deep history of the Lenape people in their ancestral homelands and the profound impacts of displacement through colonial expansion and forced removal. It traces how, despite these disruptions, the Lenape have sustained cultural continuity through language, traditions, and the transmission of knowledge across generations.The presentation highlights ongoing efforts to return - physically, culturally, and spiritually – to Lënapehokink, emphasizing initiatives that restore presence, strengthen community ties, and renew relationships to land. Centering both historical realities and contemporary leadership, it underscores how resilience and acts of return continue to shape Lenape identity and future pathways grounded in sovereignty, connection, and renewal.

Jeremy Johnson is the cultural education director of the Delaware Tribe of Indians, based in Bartlesville, Okla. He is Lenape, Absentee Shawnee, and Peoria. Prior to his current role, he served as assistant chief of the Delaware Tribe. A lifelong educator, he is committed to preserving and revitalizing Lenape culture and language for the future generations of his tribe. 

All events are sponsored by the Swarthmore College President’s Office.

Past events:


First event:
April 1 – Anatomy of a Shadow Economy: Body Snatching and the Making of American Medicine

 

Second event:
April 15 – Old Science Hall Renaming Task Force: Our Charge and Process, Community Survey Results, and Q&A

Third event:
April 22 – “What’s in a (Building’s) Name?”

description: This event aims to allow students to identify their goals for the year while encouraging healthy habits to manage stress. Afterward, students can pet, play with, and destress with affectionate therapy dogs.

title: Swarthmore Night at Lincoln Center Theater: Ragtime
description:
An unforgettable story, told on one of New York's greatest stages.

Swarthmore Night at Lincoln Center Theater: Ragtime

6:15 p.m.

Lincoln Center Theater
150 W 65th Street
New York
$69 per person

This event has reached capacity. To be added to a waitlist, please contact alumni@swarthmore.edu.

We’re coming together to experience Ragtime, a breathtaking musical adaptation of E.L. Doctorow’s celebrated novel. The story beautifully weaves together the lives of three fictional families — a Black pianist and his beloved; a Jewish immigrant and his daughter; and a wealthy white family — all in pursuit of the American Dream at the dawn of the 20th century.

Space for this event is extremely limited, and we expect it to fill up quickly. If you're thinking about joining us, don't wait — reserve your discounted loge ticket today.


Program

6:15 p.m. — Lobby Opens: Follow the signage to meet fellow Swatties in our private gathering area, the Patron Room, to collect your tickets and name tags before the show. Tickets will be available in this room until showtime.

6:30 p.m. — Mix, Mingle, & Learn: We'll have a special opportunity to hear from Jenna Clark Embrey, LCT's Literary Manager and Dramaturg, in conversation with Kathy Liu ’04, LCT’s Director of Development.

7:00 p.m. — Showtime: Take your seats for Ragtime. The performance runs 2 hours and 50 minutes, including one intermission.

title: Campus Culture & Climate Survey Report Listening Session: Faculty
description:
Vice President for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Brooke Vick recently shared the results of Swarthmore’s Campus Culture & Climate Survey with our community. The survey captured student, faculty, and staff perspectives on living, learning and working at Swarthmore — you can learn more and find the full report on the Campus Climate Survey website.

 

What do faculty members think about the survey findings? Do they reflect your experience at the College? What would you like to see come out of this survey, as we plan together for the future of Swarthmore?

 

Faculty are invited to join members of the Committee on Access, Inclusion, and Community (CAIC) to share your thoughts on survey findings, and what could come next.
location: McCabe LibLab, Full Space, Conference Room

title: Fetter Chamber Music Concert
description:
The Elizabeth Pollard Fetter Chamber Music Program and the Department of Music present student musicians from the Elizabeth Pollard Fetter Program in a series of chamber music concerts. 

The Elizabeth Pollard Fetter Chamber Music Program has been an integral part of the Swarthmore College performance program for four decades. It began in 1975 as the ""Pollard Scholarship Funds,"" with an initial contribution from Elizabeth Pollard Fetter '25 in memory of her mother, Emilie Garrett Pollard '93. With the passing of Elizabeth Pollard Fetter in 1977, husband Frank Whitson Fetter '20 stepped forward to endow the Elizabeth Pollard Fetter String Quartet Scholarships at Swarthmore College. Dozens of student musicians were subsequently supported in their desire to explore the string quartet repertoire.

In 2001, the program was renamed the Elizabeth Pollard Fetter Chamber Music Program and was expanded to support the coaching of multiple chamber music groups. Funding continues to be enhanced by successive generations of the Fetter family, including Bob Fetter ’53, Tom Fetter ’56, Ellen Fetter, and Annie Fetter ’88. Swarthmore College, students and faculty past and present, and the community at large, has been enriched by the musical communication made possible through the generosity of the Fetter family.

This performance is free and open to the public.  Seating is first come first served. This performance will be streamed live.

title: Senior Honors Thesis Staged Reading
description:
Spring 2026 Honors Playwriting Thesis 

The Night of Ended Secrets by Frank Paz (‘26)

Directed by Andrew Watring

Jamie and Carmine, two estranged childhood friends, reconnect as they sit vigil for the death of Jamie’s grandmother. As Jamie struggles to write his eulogy, he must choose: keep a secret out of love for his grandmother or risk living his authentic truth. Written by Frank Paz (‘26), The Night of Ended Secrets pits familial obligation against burning desire against the setting of one family’s grief.  

Performance at 8:00 p.m.

Martin Hall 028 - Ping and Rose Chen Screening Room

title: SwatWalks: Oakland
description:
Step outside and connect with your local community through SwatWalks, our new alumni-led series of outdoor social strolls! We are kicking off our very first outing in the Bay Area with a walk around Oakland's Lake Merritt.

Join host Margaret Huang '87 and fellow alumni for our inaugural SwatWalks: Oakland — a casual, family-friendly walk around scenic Lake Merritt, the nation's first wildlife refuge.

Join this easy 3-mile walk, which is perfect for all ages and abilities. Whether you’re seeking fresh air, lively conversation, or just a great reason to get outside, this is your morning. Afterward, join us for an optional brunch and music at Lake Chalet. Bring your sunscreen and your Swat spirit — let's walk, talk, and enjoy the spring.

Lake Chalet
1520 Lakeside Dr
Oakland, CA 94612

Sign up, RSVP here!

title: Swarthmore College Chinese Music Ensemble
description:
The Chinese Music Ensemble, co-directed by Guowei Wang and Lei Ouyang, performs traditional and contemporary music from different regions of China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, and the Chinese Diaspora.

Students perform on traditional Chinese instruments including the guzheng (zither), erhu (bowed fiddle), pipa (plucked lute), yangqin (hammered dulcimer), dizi (flute), and percussion. 

This performance will be streamed live.

description: Now in its 8th iteration, the TriCo Digital Scholarship Research Institute is an intensive, cohort-based learning program developed and taught by the TriCo Libraries Digital Scholarship Group. Through instensive workshops, discussions, and project showcases, participants gain experience with fundamental digital scholarship tools and methods but also explore project support and grant opportunities in the Trico and beyond, crafting potential projects and digital assignments, and wayfinding through the maze of possible tools, services, and platforms for digital research. ds-pages.swarthmore.edu…

title: Garnet Day
description:
Join our annual tradition of celebrating Swarthmore seniors during May’s Garnet Day! Please note that, since Thursday is the first day of exams, Garnet Day will be on Wednesday this month.

Garnet Day Celebration of the Class of 2026

Wednesday, May 6

4-6 p.m.

Pearson Lawn

There will be carnival games, music, and an array of snacks, including treats from Weckerly’s, Lil’ Pop Shop, and the Dia Dolce cupcake truck. Vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free food options will be available. Everyone’s favorite feathered friend, Phineas, will also make an appearance! 

We hope you can stop by to congratulate our seniors on their remarkable accomplishments, and wish them a wonderful future ahead. As always, remember to wear your Swarthmore gear to show your spirit!

title: Breakfast Cooking Class
description: Join The Swarthmore Chef team for a fun, fast-paced cooking class that teaches the essential skills every beginner needs. In just one hour, participants will learn how to cook perfect eggs, make quick and tasty microwave meals, and build confidence with basic kitchen techniques. The class is upbeat, hands-on, and designed to show that great cooking doesn’t require fancy tools. Students will pick up practical tips they can use immediately in their daily routines.

title: Swat Talk: Laura Hirai `22
description:
In a League of Her Own:
A Conversation with

Women’s Baseball Pioneer Laura Hirai ’22

Please join Laura for a conversation with Ana Apostoleris Rivera ’13 about her pioneering experiences and her insights on the future of women's baseball.

RSVP here

As a young girl, Laura Hirai ’22 fell in love with baseball. Growing up in Japan, she recalls her experience with the sport was not particularly gendered. It was not until she attempted to attend a baseball camp in the US, and was told it was only for boys, that she realized she may face some obstacles to achieving her diamond dreams. Like many Swarthmore students, Laura approached these obstacles as a challenge rather than a deterrent, and at 26 years old has become a global face of women's baseball. Her resume includes being the first, and thus far only, woman to represent Great Britain in U18 baseball; becoming the first "ball girl" for the New York Yankees during the first Major League Baseball game played in England in 2019; and being drafted in the fifth round in the inaugural draft of the US-based Women's Professional Baseball League, which is set to begin play in August. Laura also played varsity softball at Swarthmore.

Laura Hirai ’22 graduated with a Bachelor of Arts in Arabic and Spanish. A dual-sport athlete at the international level, she is also the first person to represent Great Britain in both baseball and softball simultaneously at the national level, pitching at the European Baseball Championships in 2015 and 2017. One of only three European athletes selected in the inaugural Women's Professional Baseball League draft, Laura most recently helped Great Britain claim gold in women's baseball and bronze in softball at the European Championships, with both teams earning World Cup berths.

SwatTalks is an Alumni Council initiative that engages the broader Swarthmore community through webinars featuring alumni and faculty members excelling in their fields. Each hour-long online seminar is free and open to Swarthmore alumni, families, faculty, staff, students, and friends. We welcome suggestions for future speakers and topics — please email your ideas to alumni@swarthmore.edu.

title: Campus Culture & Climate Survey Report Listening Sessions: Staff
description:
Vice President for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Brooke Vick recently shared the results of Swarthmore’s Campus Culture & Climate Survey with our community. The survey captured student, faculty, and staff perspectives on living, learning and working at Swarthmore — you can learn more and find the full report on the Campus Climate Survey website.



What do you think about the survey findings? Do they reflect your experience at the College? What would you like to see come out of this survey, as we plan together for the future of Swarthmore?

Meals for ALL in-person sessions are covered by the Office of DEI.

If you can’t make these sessions, you can attend another one of the sessions listed on the Events page of the Campus Climate Survey website (please log in with your Swarthmore credentials to view), share your input through our feedback form, or email culturesurvey@swarthmore.edu.

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: SwatPDX Book Club
description:
We’re on our 4th SwatPDX book club meeting and would love to have you join us on Tuesday, May 12, 7-9 p.m. The book selection is Train Dreams by Denis Johnson. The meeting address is an alum’s Portland home in Raleigh Hills and will be sent to attendees a few days before the meeting.
 
Please RSVP for the book club by May 5. The form includes a section to indicate your interest in future book club meetings and to receive communications about other SwatPDX events, even if you can’t attend the May book club meeting.

We hope to see you at one of our events!

Sincerely,

Ana Cragnolino ’93, Catherine Rose ’97, and Becky Voorheis ’93
SwatPDX co-leads

title: Peter van de Kamp Observatory Open House
description:
The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room

title: Philadelphia Connection Woodmere Art Museum Tour
description:
Woodmere Art Museum Tour

Charles Knox Smith Hall
9201 Germantown Avenue
Philadelphia

$15 per person
RSVP Today

This landmark exhibition celebrates the fifty-year career of former Swarthmore professor Syd Carpenter. Together, we will explore her evocative ceramic sculptures that weave together African American history, ancestral lands, and the beauty of the natural world. It is a wonderful opportunity to engage with profound art while reconnecting with your local alumni community. Then join us for an optional group lunch after the tour to continue our conversations. Lunch venue details will be shared very soon.

Warmly,
The Philadelphia Connection Committee

description:
Join our annual tradition of celebrating Swarthmore seniors during May’s Garnet Day! Please note that, since Thursday is the first day of exams, Garnet Day will be on Wednesday this month.

Garnet Day Celebration of the Class of 2026

Wednesday, May 6

4-6 p.m.

Pearson Lawn

There will be carnival games, music, and an array of snacks, including treats from Weckerly’s, Lil’ Pop Shop, and the Dia Dolce cupcake truck. Vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free food options will be available. Everyone’s favorite feathered friend, Phineas, will also make an appearance! 

We hope you can stop by to congratulate our seniors on their remarkable accomplishments, and wish them a wonderful future ahead. As always, remember to wear your Swarthmore gear to show your spirit!

The Physics and Astronomy department hosts a telescope open house on the second Tuesday of each month at the Peter van de Kamp Observatory on the roof of the Science Center. Directions are available at www.swarthmore.edu/peter-van-de-kamp-observatory/directions.

The open house hours are 8:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. during standard time and 9:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m. during daylight savings time.

If the weather is bad, the open house will be cancelled and an announcement will be posted on the Physics and Astronomy department website and the College calendar. Check https://www.swarthmore.edu/physics-astronomy on the day of the open house if the weather is questionable

Open to the public.
location: Science Center Peter Van de Kamp Observatory, Science Center Observatory Computer Room

title: American Communities 1776-2026
description: Swarthmore College Libraries marks "America250" with a special exhibition in McCabe Library. Drawing on art and artifacts from Special Collections, the exhibition explores how family, land, and nation have shaped the idea of “America,” and invites visitors to consider how they see this country and its history today.

title: Pilates with Cathy Raymond
description:
Pilates is a progressive system of exercises designed to stretch, strengthen, and balance both mind and body. Benefits include increased core strength, better posture and balance skills, more physical and mental energy, and improved sports performance.

A recording of each class is available on YouTube.

title: Hatha Yoga with Stacy Bomento
description: This Hatha yoga class will focus on breathing and mindful movement, helping to increase strength and flexibility. Offering modifications for various postures, this class is accessible to all levels of practice. Each practice will include poses, breathing exercises, and a restorative and calming finish, followed by guided meditation. **There will be no class on July 23**

title: Group Circuit Training with Chris
description: Do you struggle to figure out which workout to do at the gym, or do you need some extra motivation? If so, this class is for you. Receive personal attention to help you improve your overall fitness through a total body workout. The class will offer modifications or challenges to suit each participant. Missed a workout? Review past workouts online.
